title: Structure-aware git merge driver + post-merge integrity pass — pi-context plugs into git's 3-way merge so substrate files merge data-model-valid, not line-mangled
context: FGAP-004: substrate files fall through to git's line-based merge and get mangled. Git owns branches, commits, merge-base, refs, and invocation; pi-context supplies only the structure-aware per-file reconcile plus a whole-substrate post-merge pass. No native version DAG, snapshot roots, or branch-pinning — git provides all of that.
decision: 1) Register a git merge driver: .gitattributes maps substrate files (<block>.json, relations.json, config.json, schemas) to merge=pi-context, with a [merge "pi-context"] driver entry. Git computes the merge base and invokes the driver per file with %O (ancestor) / %A (ours) / %B (theirs) / %P (path). 2) Per-file structure-aware 3-way: parse the three versions; match items by stable id/oid (never array index) — added-on-one -> include; removed-on-one & untouched-on-other -> remove; modified-on-one -> take; modified-on-both -> field-level merge; removed-on-one & modified-on-other -> conflict. 3) Field-kind-dispatched field merge: scalar/object -> the side changed vs base (both-identical -> take; both-different -> declared deterministic policy or conflict); SEQUENCE fields -> the convergent ordered-sequence field-type (FGAP-005) run on the field's three versions. 4) Edges (relations.json) merge by 3-way set ops on (parent, child, relation_type) triples. 5) objects/: distinct content-hash-named files don't collide; git's directory merge unions them (equal hash == equal content) — no driver needed. 6) config/schema 3-way; divergent redefinition of the same block_kind / relation_type / schema -> surfaced conflict. 7) Post-merge pass (git post-merge hook OR a pi-context merge-finalize command — a per-file driver cannot see across files): cross-block referential integrity (drop or conflict edges whose endpoints were deleted in another file) + full declared-invariant + lens-validator revalidation (branch-valid states can merge to globally invalid). 8) Typed structured conflict set ({item_id, field, base, ours, theirs} + edge/schema analogues), surfaced by the post-merge pass, not inline text markers. 9) Branches, merge-base, commit lineage, refs, and cherry-pick remain git's — not reimplemented; the driver only makes git's per-file result data-model-valid.
consequences: No native version DAG, snapshot roots, branch refs, or merge-base finder — git supplies all of them. Branch-pinning is not needed and is not filed.

content_parent / content_hash keep their existing content-addressing + drift roles; merge does NOT use them for base-finding (git's %O is the base). Item-matching inside the driver uses the stable id/oid.
The merge driver + per-file merge logic + field-kind dispatch live in block-api; the cross-block-integrity + invariant revalidation pass is a git post-merge hook / merge-finalize command.
The convergent ordered-sequence field-type (FGAP-005) is the field-kind invoked inside the driver on %O/%A/%B — this is its host.
Scope is same-repo, shared-history branch merges; cross-project, no-shared-history import is clone (DEC-0002), an orthogonal axis git cannot merge.
